body:
## Summary

Re-verified #680 and #681 against `v0.289.0` (both closed as fixed). **#682 is genuinely fixed and stays closed.** But:

- **#680 is only partially fixed.** `web_search`/`web_fetch` now fire (they were 0/50), but the actual complaint — *routing is phrasing-gated, not intent-based* — still reproduces. The fix appears to have added the specific phrasings from the original repro rather than generalizing to intent.
- **#681 still reproduces verbatim** on the exact class of input it was closed for.

Re-tested live on `v0.289.0`: 160 runs through the real CLIs (`with-formal-ai --non-interactive`) with a logging proxy recording emitted `tool_call`s, cross-validated against 60 direct server-level calls. The two harnesses agree cell-for-cell.

## Correction to the original #680 report

My original report claimed a 5×6 matrix with many 0/10 cells. **That methodology was partly wrong and I want to correct the record:** each CLI advertises a *different tool vocabulary*, and many cells scored 0/10 for a tool the CLI **never advertised** — that was never a server bug.

Tools actually advertised (captured from live `request_tools`):

| CLI | advertises |
|---|---|
| agent | `websearch`, `webfetch`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `read` |
| opencode | `webfetch`, `write`, `edit`, `bash`, `read` |
| codex | `exec_command` only |
| qwen | `web_fetch`, `read_file` |
| gemini | `google_web_search`, `read_file` |

Only **15 of 30** cells are testable. Note also names differ (`websearch` vs `web_search`) — a naive exact-name check under-counts. Everything below scores only real cells, using per-CLI names.

## Results on v0.289.0 — correct `tool_call` emitted / runs

| CLI | web_search | web_fetch | write | edit | shell | read |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| agent | 6/10 | 2/10 | 3/10 | 2/10 | 7/10 | 4/10 |
| opencode | n/a | 2/10 | 3/10 | 2/10 | 4/10 | 4/10 |
| codex | n/a | n/a | n/a | n/a | 7/10 | n/a |
| qwen | n/a | 2/10 | n/a | n/a | n/a | 5/10 |
| gemini | 6/10 | n/a | n/a | n/a | n/a | 5/10 |

**Per-tool:** `web_search` 12/20 · `web_fetch` 6/30 · `write` 6/20 · `edit` 4/20 · `shell` 18/30 · `read` 18/40

Real progress vs 0.282.0: `web_search` 0 → 12/20, `web_fetch` 0 → 6/30, `edit` 0 → 4/20. But every tool still fails the majority of natural phrasings, and identical rates across all five CLIs and three protocol surfaces point to one shared planner root cause.

## #680: still phrasing-gated

Semantically identical requests, opposite outcomes — e.g. `web_fetch` (agent CLI):

| phrasing | result |
|---|---|
| `fetch https://example.com` | ✅ `webfetch` |
| `download the page at https://example.com` | ✅ `webfetch` |
| `get the contents of https://example.com` | ❌ none |
| `retrieve https://example.com` | ❌ none |
| `open https://example.com and tell me what it says` | ❌ none |
| `load https://example.com` | ❌ none |
| `visit https://example.com and summarize it` | ❌ none |

Same for `web_search`: `search the web for rust async` ✅ but `google what is a monad` ❌, `what does the web say about serde` ❌, `I need current info from the internet on axum` ❌.

`edit` is worst (4/20) — only `edit main.rs and change foo to bar` / `in main.rs replace foo with bar` land; `update`, `modify`, `patch`, `rewrite`, `substitute`, `refactor` all emit nothing.

## #681: still reproduces

The closed issue was *"a file-creation request emits a `read` tool_call on the nonexistent target instead of `write`"*. Verbatim on `v0.289.0`:

```
agent      "new file: notes.txt, contents: hello"  -> read     (expected: write)
opencode   "new file: notes.txt, contents: hello"  -> read     (expected: write)
```

Still the wrong tool, not merely a missing one. The fix handles `create a file named X with the content Y` but not other file-creation phrasings.

## Reproduction

Server-level (no CLI needed) — **note `--agent-mode` is now required**, without it the server returns a `policy_refusal` and no tool calls:

```bash
formal-ai serve --port 8080 --agent-mode

curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8080/v1/chat/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"model":"formal-ai",
       "tools":[{"type":"function","function":{"name":"web_fetch","description":"Fetch a URL",
         "parameters":{"type":"object","properties":{"url":{"type":"string"}},"required":["url"]}}}],
       "messages":[{"role":"user","content":"get the contents of https://example.com"}]}' \
  | jq '.choices[0].message.tool_calls'
```

**Actual:** `null` (plain prose reply). **Expected:** a `web_fetch` call.
Swap the message for `fetch https://example.com` and it fires — same intent, same tools, different phrasing.

## Suggested direction

The fixes for #680/#681 look like added surface phrasings rather than intent generalization, so the failure class survives. Route on **intent** (verb-class + argument shape: a bare URL ⇒ fetch; `<path>` + content ⇒ write; `<path>` + old/new ⇒ edit) rather than matching phrase templates. A table-driven test over N paraphrases per tool — asserting *all* paraphrases route identically, not just the canonical one — would prevent the next regression.

## Verified fixed (FYI)

**#682** — explicit `"content": null` on assistant tool-call turns now returns **HTTP 200** (was 400 `MessageContent`); qwen completed 20 live runs with zero protocol errors. Correctly closed.

---
Environment: `formal-ai 0.289.0` (cargo), macOS 24.6.0; agent 0.24.1, opencode 1.17.19, codex 0.144.1, qwen 0.7.1, gemini 0.50.0.


Related: #680, #681 (closed, re-opened by this evidence), #682 (verified fixed), #687 (adjacent: generalization of web search/fetch + natural-language actions), #711 (changelog pipeline — why these fixes are invisible in release notes).

## 🚨 Solution Draft Failed

The automated solver stopped before creating a pull request, so no PR was opened for this issue.

### Failure
- **Repository**: `link-assistant/formal-ai`
- **Issue**: #712
- **Tool**: `agent`
- **Requested model**: `formal-ai`

**Reason**
```text
Invalid model name
```

### What you can do
- Resolve the repository, account, permissions, or environment problem described above, then rerun the solver.
- Repository owner or Hive Mind administrator path: handle manual recreation or fix of the repository when the required action is outside the requester access.

Logs were not attached because `--attach-logs` was not enabled.

title: Regression: #680 tool routing still phrasing-gated and #681 write→read still reproduces on v0.289.0 (160-run matrix)
